title: VehicleState
description: Canonical vehicle state as consumed by MyRoboTaxi SDKs. Defines every field name, type, unit, nullability, atomic group membership, and data classification. Both the WebSocket vehicle_update messages and the REST snapshot endpoint return subsets of this schema.

vehicleId.description: Opaque database identifier (cuid). NOT the VIN. All SDK API calls use this ID (FR-4.2).

model.description: Vehicle model (e.g., 'Model 3', 'Model Y'). SPEC-ONLY until MYR-24: not currently loaded by the Go Vehicle struct in internal/store/types.go. Will be nullable until MYR-24 lands; consumers MUST tolerate null.

year.description: Model year. SPEC-ONLY until MYR-24: not currently loaded by the Go Vehicle struct in internal/store/types.go. Will be nullable until MYR-24 lands; consumers MUST tolerate null.

color.description: Vehicle exterior color. SPEC-ONLY until MYR-24: not currently loaded by the Go Vehicle struct in internal/store/types.go. Will be nullable until MYR-24 lands; consumers MUST tolerate null.

status.description: Derived vehicle status. Part of the gear atomic group -- updated atomically with gearPosition. When gearPosition is D or R, status is 'driving'; otherwise 'parked'. The 'charging', 'offline', and 'in_service' states are set by server-side logic.

speed.description: Current vehicle speed. Rounded to nearest integer from Tesla's float emission.

heading.description: Compass heading. 0 = North, 90 = East, 180 = South, 270 = West.

latitude.description: Current GPS latitude. Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Non-nullable: on initial connect before the first GPS fix, latitude/longitude default to 0. Per the '0,0 = no fix' convention (§2.3), SDK consumers MUST treat latitude == 0 && longitude == 0 as 'no position available' rather than a valid location in the Gulf of Guinea.

longitude.description: Current GPS longitude. Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Non-nullable: on initial connect before the first GPS fix, latitude/longitude default to 0. Per the '0,0 = no fix' convention (§2.3), SDK consumers MUST treat latitude == 0 && longitude == 0 as 'no position available' rather than a valid location in the Gulf of Guinea.

locationName.description: Reverse-geocoded place name for current location (e.g., 'Home', 'Whole Foods Market'). Derived from GPS coordinates server-side. SPEC-ONLY until MYR-24: not currently loaded by the Go Vehicle struct in internal/store/types.go. Will be nullable until MYR-24 lands; consumers MUST tolerate null.

locationAddress.description: Reverse-geocoded street address for current location. Derived from GPS coordinates server-side. SPEC-ONLY until MYR-24: not currently loaded by the Go Vehicle struct in internal/store/types.go. Will be nullable until MYR-24 lands; consumers MUST tolerate null.

gearPosition.description: Transmission gear position. Null when vehicle is asleep or gear has not yet been reported. When gearPosition changes, status is re-derived and both are delivered together.

chargeLevel.description: Battery state of charge. Rounded to nearest integer from Tesla's float emission. Maps from Tesla SOC or BatteryLevel fields.

chargeState.description: Charge state enum from Tesla proto field 2 (ChargeState). Added as a v1 charge atomic group member by MYR-11 (see websocket-protocol.md §4.1.4 and §10 DV-03). Nullable until the DV-03 server wiring lands — SDK consumers MUST tolerate null and render a neutral placeholder (not a spinner). Once wired, the charge atomic group will arrive as a four-field batch alongside chargeLevel, estimatedRange, and timeToFull. Nullability is expressed via the union `type: ["string", "null"]`; the `enum` list intentionally contains only the string values so that naive JSON Schema validators (some Go and Python libraries coerce `null` out of mixed-type enums) behave consistently. A null value validates against the type clause and is exempt from the enum constraint per JSON Schema draft 2020-12.

estimatedRange.description: Estimated remaining driving range based on current charge. Rounded to nearest integer.

timeToFull.description: Estimated time to full charge at the current charging rate. Added as a v1 charge atomic group member by MYR-11 (see websocket-protocol.md §4.1.4 and §10 DV-04). Tesla proto field 43 (TimeToFullCharge, double). Unit is HOURS (decimal, fractional values supported — e.g. 1.5 for 90 minutes) per the tesla-fleet-telemetry-sme skill and the legacy Tesla REST API; empirical verification via charging-vehicle protobuf capture is tracked as DV-17 in websocket-protocol.md §10. Nullable until the DV-04 server wiring lands — SDK consumers MUST tolerate null and render a neutral placeholder (not a spinner). Once wired, 0 (or null when disconnected) means no active charging session.

interiorTemp.description: Cabin interior temperature. Rounded to nearest integer.

exteriorTemp.description: Ambient exterior temperature. Rounded to nearest integer.

odometerMiles.description: Total odometer reading. Rounded to nearest integer.

fsdMilesToday.description: Full Self-Driving miles driven since last counter reset (typically daily). SPEC-ONLY until MYR-24: not currently loaded by the Go Vehicle struct in internal/store/types.go. Will be nullable until MYR-24 lands; consumers MUST tolerate null.

destinationName.description: Name of the active navigation destination. Null when no navigation is active. When navigation is cancelled, this field and all other navigation group fields are atomically cleared to null.

destinationAddress.description: Street address of the active navigation destination. Null when no navigation is active. SPEC-ONLY until MYR-24: not currently loaded by the Go Vehicle struct in internal/store/types.go; will always be null in snapshots until MYR-24 lands.

destinationLatitude.description: Latitude of the navigation destination. Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Null when no navigation is active.

destinationLongitude.description: Longitude of the navigation destination. Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Null when no navigation is active.

originLatitude.description: Latitude of the navigation trip origin. Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Null when no navigation is active.

originLongitude.description: Longitude of the navigation trip origin. Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Null when no navigation is active.

etaMinutes.description: Estimated time of arrival in minutes remaining. Null when no navigation is active. Rounded to nearest integer.

tripDistanceRemaining.description: Remaining distance to navigation destination. Null when no navigation is active.

navRouteCoordinates.description: Active navigation route as an array of [longitude, latitude] coordinate pairs (GeoJSON/Mapbox order). Decoded from Tesla's RouteLine (Base64-encoded protobuf wrapping a Google Encoded Polyline at 1e6 precision). Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Null when no navigation is active.

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent telemetry update applied to this vehicle state.

navigation.description: Navigation state delivered as a single atomic unit. When any navigation field changes, the server accumulates all sibling nav fields within a 500ms debounce window (NFR-3.2) and delivers them together. When navigation is cancelled, ALL fields in this group are atomically cleared to null (FR-2.3).
consistency-predicates: If destinationLatitude is non-null, then destinationLongitude MUST also be non-null, and vice versa.
If originLatitude is non-null, then originLongitude MUST also be non-null, and vice versa.
If destinationName is non-null, then destinationLatitude, destinationLongitude, and navRouteCoordinates MUST also be non-null (NFR-3.3).
If any navigation field is null, ALL navigation fields MUST be null (atomic clear on nav cancel, FR-2.3).
etaMinutes and tripDistanceRemaining MAY be null independently during the 500ms accumulation window, but the DB snapshot MUST have all-or-nothing consistency.
navigation.nullability: All fields nullable. Null = no active navigation.

charge.description: Battery and charge state delivered together. When any charge field changes, sibling fields are included in the same update message. v1 expands this group to four fields per MYR-11 — chargeState and timeToFull are transitional-nullable until the DV-03 / DV-04 server wiring lands (see websocket-protocol.md §10).
consistency-predicates: chargeLevel and estimatedRange MUST both be present in the DB snapshot (NFR-3.5). They are non-nullable with default 0.
chargeState and timeToFull are transitional-nullable until DV-03 / DV-04 wiring ships. Consumers MUST tolerate null for both on any frame; once wired, both fields MUST be present in the DB snapshot.

chargeLevel: 0 is context-dependent. With status == 'offline', the UI SHOULD interpret chargeLevel: 0 as 'unknown' (no telemetry received yet, DB default). With status == 'driving' or 'parked' (i.e., the vehicle is online), chargeLevel: 0 SHOULD be interpreted as 'critical' or 'empty battery'. See §2.2 of vehicle-state-schema.md.
charge.nullability: chargeLevel and estimatedRange are non-nullable (default 0 on initial vehicle creation). chargeState and timeToFull are transitional-nullable until server wiring lands.

gps.description: GPS position and orientation delivered together. On initial connect before the first GPS fix, latitude and longitude may be 0 (DB default).
consistency-predicates: latitude and longitude MUST always be present together (NFR-3.3).
heading MUST be present whenever latitude/longitude are present.
Values of 0,0 for latitude/longitude represent the DB default (no GPS fix yet), not a valid location.
gps.nullability: Non-nullable in DB (default 0). SDK consumers SHOULD treat 0,0 as 'no fix'.

gear.description: Gear position and derived vehicle status delivered together. When gearPosition changes, the server derives status (driving/parked) and includes both fields in the same message.
consistency-predicates: When gearPosition is 'D' or 'R', status MUST be 'driving'.
When gearPosition is 'P' or 'N', status MUST be 'parked' (unless overridden by charging/offline/in_service logic).
status is always present; gearPosition is nullable (null = vehicle asleep or gear not yet reported).
gear.nullability: gearPosition is nullable. status is non-nullable (default 'offline').
